question: Is the hot air balloon depicted as a miniature object, appearing significantly smaller than the dandelion seed structure to imply it is being carried?
Is the hot air balloon positioned directly beneath the dandelion seed?
Is there a visible string or tether physically connecting the dandelion seed to the hot air balloon?
Does the dandelion seed appear to be airborne or floating (separated from the main seed head)?
Does the balloon retain the characteristic red color and vertical gore (panel) design from the reference image?
Is the wicker basket structure of the balloon visible and correctly positioned beneath the envelope?
Is the carrier object clearly recognizable as a dandelion seed with delicate pappus filaments?
Does the lighting on the miniaturized balloon match the soft, ambient direction and intensity of the macro scene?
Is the focus and depth-of-field of the balloon consistent with the dandelion seed it is attached to (e.g., matching blur/sharpness)?
Does the background maintain the green, macro-photography bokeh style of the second reference image (avoiding elements from the balloon's original background)?
Is the image free from obvious compositing artifacts (e.g., halos, jagged edges) where the balloon and string attach to the seed?
Is the balloon free from severe geometric distortion (e.g., crushed shape or warped panels)?
Is there exactly one hot air balloon present in the image (no duplicate balloons)?
